Tests for the private NumPy argument parsing functionality.
They mainly exists to ensure good test coverage without having to try the
weirder cases on actual numpy functions but test them in one place.
The test function is defined in C to be equivalent to (errors may not always
match exactly, and could be adjusted):
    def func(arg1, /, arg2, *, arg3):
        i = integer(arg1)  # reproducing the 'i' parsing in Python.
        return None
argparse_example_functionc
Nz$integer argument expected, got float
matchg
pytest
raises
	TypeError
func
OverflowError
D/usr/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/numpy/core/tests/test_argparse.py
test_invalid_integers
)	Nz&missing required positional argument 0r
arg2
arg3z,missing required argument \'arg2\' \(pos 1\)
test_missing_arguments
Nz7takes from 2 to 3 positional arguments but 4 were givenr
test_too_many_positional(
Nz4given by name \('arg2'\) and position \(position 1\)r
test_multiple_values/
missing_argr
z0got an unexpected keyword argument 'missing_arg'r
unicode_r
test_string_fallbacks5
__doc__r
numpyr
numpy.core._multiarray_testsr
<module>
